input: Recommend first-line antihypertensive therapy for a 55yo Black male with stage 2 hypertension (BP 162/98), no comorbidities, no drug allergies.

expected_details.population_specific: ACE inhibitors/ARBs less effective as monotherapy in Black patients; thiazide or CCB preferred
expected_details.should_not_recommend: ACE/ARB as first-line monotherapy for this patient (population-specific guidance)

input: Select an SSRI for a 35yo female with major depressive disorder, first episode. She is also taking tramadol for chronic back pain. No other medications.

expected_details.preferred_ssri: avoid strong CYP2D6 inhibitors (paroxetine, fluoxetine) due to tramadol interaction
expected_details.interaction_concern: SSRIs + tramadol = serotonin syndrome risk; also CYP2D6 inhibition reduces tramadol → O-desmethyltramadol conversion
expected_details.additional_concern: consider tapering tramadol if possible — dual serotonergic risk

input: Recommend second-line therapy for a 60yo male with T2DM (A1c 8.2% on metformin 2000mg), BMI 34, history of heart failure with reduced ejection fraction (HFrEF). eGFR 55.

expected_details.rationale: SGLT2i have proven HF benefit (EMPEROR-Reduced, DAPA-HF), weight loss, and renal protection
should_not_recommend: GLP-1 RA as first add-on (HF benefit less established than SGLT2i for HFrEF)
expected_details.renal_consideration: eGFR 55 — SGLT2i still effective for cardiorenal benefits at this eGFR

input: Select an oral anticoagulant for a 72yo female with non-valvular atrial fibrillation. CHA₂DS₂-VASc score 4 (age, female, hypertension, diabetes). CrCl 40 mL/min. No prior bleeding.

specific_recommendations.apixaban: 2.5 mg BID (dose reduced for ≥2 of: age ≥80, weight ≤60kg, Cr ≥1.5)
specific_recommendations.dabigatran: caution at CrCl 30-50; 150mg BID or 110mg BID depending on guidelines

expected_details.renal_dosing: CrCl 40 requires dose adjustment for some DOACs
expected_details.warfarin_considerations: acceptable alternative if DOAC cost prohibitive; INR target 2-3

input: A 78yo patient is on: simvastatin 40mg, amlodipine 10mg, diltiazem 240mg, metformin 1000mg, warfarin. Identify all clinically significant interactions.

critical_interactions.mechanism: CYP3A4 inhibition by diltiazem increases simvastatin levels — rhabdomyolysis risk
critical_interactions.action: switch simvastatin to pravastatin or rosuvastatin, or limit simvastatin to 10mg

critical_interactions.mechanism: CYP3A4 inhibition — limit simvastatin to 20mg with amlodipine
additive hypotension and bradycardia risk (two CCBs)
critical_interactions.action: monitor BP/HR; may need to choose one CCB

expected_details.triple_interaction: simvastatin + diltiazem + amlodipine = compounded CYP3A4 inhibition — high rhabdomyolysis risk

input: A hospital formulary restricts brand-name esomeprazole. Recommend a therapeutic alternative for a patient currently on esomeprazole 40mg for GERD.

expected_details.therapeutic_equivalence: all PPIs are generally equivalent for GERD at equipotent doses
expected_details.cost_consideration: omeprazole is cheapest (generic OTC); pantoprazole has fewest drug interactions (CYP2C19)
expected_details.special_situations: if patient on clopidogrel → prefer pantoprazole (least CYP2C19 inhibition)

input: Check contraindications: Can a patient with a history of angioedema from lisinopril take losartan? Can they take sacubitril/valsartan?

expected_details.losartan_assessment: generally acceptable — ARBs can be used cautiously after ACE-inhibitor angioedema (cross-reactivity rate ~2-8%)
expected_details.sacubitril_valsartan: CONTRAINDICATED within 36 hours of ACE inhibitor; history of ACE-inhibitor angioedema is a relative contraindication due to neprilysin inhibition increasing bradykinin
expected_details.nuance: ACE-inhibitor angioedema is bradykinin-mediated; ARBs don't affect bradykinin; sacubitril increases bradykinin
expected_details.monitoring: if ARB used after ACE-I angioedema: close monitoring for first doses

input: Calculate appropriate ibuprofen dose for a febrile 4-year-old child weighing 18 kg.

expected_details.formulation: oral suspension (100 mg/5 mL typical concentration)
expected_details.duration: use for fever; limit duration; consult if fever persists >3 days

input: Adjust all medications for a patient with CKD stage 4 (eGFR 22): gabapentin 600mg TID, metformin 1000mg BID, allopurinol 300mg daily, lisinopril 20mg daily.

expected_details.gabapentin: reduce to 300mg daily or 100-300mg after dialysis (if applicable); significant renal clearance
expected_details.metformin: contraindicated at eGFR <30 (FDA) or use with extreme caution at reduced dose; many guidelines say stop
expected_details.allopurinol: reduce to 100-200mg daily; accumulates in renal impairment → increased hypersensitivity risk
expected_details.lisinopril: generally no dose adjustment needed but monitor potassium and Cr closely; may worsen eGFR acutely

input: Recommend statin therapy for a 52yo male with LDL 185 mg/dL, no ASCVD history, no diabetes, 10-year ASCVD risk 12%. No liver disease.

expected_details.risk_category: LDL ≥190 = high-intensity statin regardless of ASCVD risk (primary prevention)
expected_details.wait: LDL 185 is <190 — falls into intermediate risk (7.5-20% 10-year risk) category
expected_details.target: ≥30-50% LDL reduction; some guidelines target LDL <100 or <70
